regions.text: Now the doctor is taking the man’s pulse. He has his finger on the man’s wrist and is counting the times his heart pumps the blood in one minute.
The doctor can feel the motion or pulsing of the blood every time the man’s heart pumps. In most men the pulse rate is between seventy and eighty times a minute. In children it is higher.
The runner’s pulse rate is 72. It will be higher when he is running. pulse: motion of the blood. pulse rate: number of pulses. wrist: end of arm. counting: discovering how many.
FUTURE: will count. PRESENT: count(s). PAST: counted.
